1226.reason: Error: should use checkSym instead of curSym to correctly check the token after the expression

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: should use checkSym instead of curSym to correctly check the token after the expression
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: should use checkSym instead of curSym to correctly check the token after the expression
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Should use checkSym instead of curSym; otherwise the check logic is incorrect, as it checks the current token instead of the pre-read token.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  This condition checks the global parser's current token (curSym) instead of the lookahead token (checkSym). This prevents the lookahead cursor (checkSym) from advancing past the closing parenthesis of a sub-expression during lookahead analysis. While this might not always cause TLE directly in correct code, it corrupts the lookahead state, which can lead to incorrect parsing decisions or infinite loops in complex expressions if the lookahead fails to synchronize properly. It should be checkSym.tokenType == RPARENT.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition checks 'curSym' (the current parsing position) instead of 'checkSym' (the lookahead position). This prevents the lookahead index from advancing past the right parenthesis, causing the lookahead mechanism in 'Stmt' to fail or loop incorrectly when distinguishing between assignments and expressions.

582.reason: IndexOutOfBoundsException when tokens list is empty (no token generated)

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  IndexOutOfBoundsException when tokens list is empty (no token generated)

590.reason: The Parser is implemented as a singleton, but its state (curIndex, curSym, etc.) is not reset when reused. If the testing environment runs multiple test cases in the same JVM, the parser will retain the state from the previous case (e.g., curIndex at the end of the token list), leading to infinite loops or incorrect parsing in subsequent cases. It should either create a new instance every time or reset the state.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The Parser is implemented as a singleton, but its state (curIndex, curSym, etc.) is not reset when reused. If the testing environment runs multiple test cases in the same JVM, the parser will retain the state from the previous case (e.g., curIndex at the end of the token list), leading to infinite loops or incorrect parsing in subsequent cases. It should either create a new instance every time or reset the state.

678.reason: [The parser may get stuck in an infinite loop here because if the current token doesn't match any of the expected patterns for declarations or function definitions, the loop conditions won't advance the current token position, causing repeated evaluation of the same condition without progress]
